raw_consumer_statement: I received my billing statement in XX/XX/XXXX from Luxury Card MasterCard, issued by Barclays, that contained 4 charges that I didn't recognize. I called Barclays ' dispute department and they readily reversed the charges whilst they were investigating the fraudulent charges. Now, yesterday, I received a letter from Barclays ( attached ) that claims that they have determined that the activity was valid because the card was " in my possession and chip card technology was used, which can not be counterfeited ''. As such, they state that they are reversing the provisional credit, which I will be required to pay. I called the Barclays dispute department again this morning and reminded them that the four ( 4 ) charges in question were made on XX/XX/XXXX in XXXX XXXX, New Jersey. Neither my wife or I have EVER been to XXXX XXXX. Also, my wife and I have not traveled to New Jersey in over 30 years. How could I have possibly made these charges? Furthermore, I told the lady at the dispute department that my wife and I were celebrating XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  ( XX/XX/XXXX ) at XXXX XXXX XXXX restaurant ( owned by XXXX XXXX ) in XXXX, FL. This can be confirmed and verified by the XXXX XXXX of the restaurant along with at least XXXX other couples that were present. The dispute department issued me a case number XXXX.
